justification: Both extractions are genuinely equivalent across all four dimensions. Both accurately capture skills demonstrated in the resume without fabricating Spark, Kafka, or ETL experience. Both extract a well-calibrated set of skills (neither bloated nor sparse). Neither misclassifies projects — though neither output explicitly labels project taxonomy categories, both correctly represent the nature of each project. Both capture the concrete numeric metrics faithfully (52% latency reduction, 98% time reduction, 83% accuracy, 5 hrs/week saved, 1,000+ orders, etc.). The main stylistic difference is that A rewrites bullets with more verbose prose while B stays closer to the original terse style, but neither approach is clearly superior for the evaluation criteria. Skill organization differs slightly (B adds a 'Data Platform & Infrastructure' category surfacing ARQ, Alembic, WebSockets, JWKS, SSE which are real demonstrated tools), which is marginally more informative, but A includes Scikit-Learn and MNE in frameworks which is equally valid. The differences are within 0-1 points on every dimension.
